title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
summary: Flash is a decorator-based framework that simplifies GPU cloud deployment from IDEs, replacing multi-step Docker and registry workflows with a single @flash.endpoint annotation. It supports hot reload for model swaps and integrates with RunPod's GPU cloud infrastructure including serverless autoscaling across data centers.
writeup: This talk introduces Flash, a decorator-based framework from RunPod that compresses GPU cloud deployment into a single @flash.endpoint annotation, replacing the usual multi-step Docker build and registry push workflow from within an IDE. It matters because it shortens the path from local code to deployed inference on RunPod's GPU cloud, including serverless autoscaling across data centers. Key takeaways: deployment collapses to one annotation, hot reload allows model swaps without a full redeploy, and scaling is handled by RunPod's serverless infrastructure.
